Possible_Values: Halogen Bulb, LED, LED with DRL, Projector LED, Bi-LED
How_To_Spot: Yellow-ish round bulb glow = Halogen; crisp white / strip = LED; lens projector pod = Projector
Example_Differentiates: Pulsar NS125 Standard (Halogen) vs Bluetooth (LED); Ntorq Drum/Disc (Halogen) vs Race/XP/XT (LED)

How_To_Spot: Check sidewall marking 'TUBELESS' / 'TUBE TYPE'; tubeless usually paired with alloy wheels
Example_Differentiates: Higher trims usually move to tubeless + alloy

How_To_Spot: Cast multi-spoke one-piece = Alloy; wire spokes = Spoke; pressed steel rim with cover = Steel
Example_Differentiates: Classic 350 ABS (Spoke) vs Gunmetal/Stealth (Alloy); Jupiter Standard (Steel) vs ZX (Alloy); Activa Standard (Steel) vs Deluxe (Alloy)

Possible_Values: Single Tone, Dual Tone, Single Tone (Fade), Chrome Tank, Blacked-out, Military / Signals theme, Dark theme, Special / Graphics edition
How_To_Spot: Read the tank/panel paint and graphics
Example_Differentiates: Hunter Metro Dapper vs Rebel; Classic Heritage vs Heritage Premium vs Signals

Possible_Values: Non-ABS, CBS, Single Channel, Dual Channel
How_To_Spot: ABS sticker/decal; ABS ring on disc; CBS = combined cable to both wheels; count ABS sensors (1 = single, 2 = dual)
Example_Differentiates: Hunter Retro Factory (Single) vs Metro (Dual); Bullet Military (Single) vs Standard (Dual)

Possible_Values: Kick Only, Electric (Self) Start Only, Kick + Electric Start
How_To_Spot: Look for an electric start button on the right switchgear and/or a kick lever
Example_Differentiates: Classic 350 KS vs ES; Bullet 350 vs Bullet 350 ES

Possible_Values: Analogue, Semi-Digital, Digital, LCD Display, TFT, SmartXonnect (Bluetooth), Tripper Navigation
How_To_Spot: Full needle dials = Analogue; needle + LCD = Semi-Digital; full screen = Digital/TFT; nav pod = Tripper
Example_Differentiates: Apache LCD vs SmartXonnect; Classic Heritage (Semi-Digital) vs Dark (Tripper)

How_To_Spot: Mechanical number drum = Analogue; LCD digits = Digital
Example_Differentiates: Tracks with Meter Type; analogue clusters carry mechanical odo

How_To_Spot: Combination of front and rear brake type (see Front/Rear Brake fields)
Example_Differentiates: Apache RM Drum (Disc-Drum) vs RM Disc (Disc-Disc)

How_To_Spot: Disc rotor visible at front wheel vs enclosed drum hub
Example_Differentiates: Ntorq Drum vs Disc; Jupiter 125 Drum vs Disc - Alloy

How_To_Spot: Disc rotor visible at rear wheel vs enclosed drum hub
Example_Differentiates: Bullet Military (Drum) vs Standard (Disc)

Possible_Values: Front Disc Only, Front + Rear Disc, No Disc (Drum-Drum)
How_To_Spot: Count visible disc rotors on the wheels
Example_Differentiates: Distinguishes drum/single-disc/double-disc trims

How_To_Spot: Smart key has no metal blade slot; proximity fob / engine button
Example_Differentiates: Activa 6G Deluxe (Standard) vs H-Smart (Smart Key)

How_To_Spot: Pillion backrest pad/rail behind the seat
Example_Differentiates: Meteor Fireball (No) vs Stellar/Aurora/Supernova (Yes); Jupiter Standard vs Classic

How_To_Spot: USB socket usually near handlebar / glovebox
Example_Differentiates: Pulsar NS125 Standard (No) vs Bluetooth (Yes)

Possible_Values: Halogen Headlamp, LED Headlamp, LED DRL, LED Tail Lamp, LED Position Lamp
How_To_Spot: Inspect headlamp, tail lamp and DRL element type
Example_Differentiates: Higher trims add LED head/tail/DRL

How_To_Spot: Amber bulb vs LED turn-signal element
Example_Differentiates: Classic Heritage (No LED) vs Dark/Chrome (LED)

Possible_Values: Standard Chrome, Blacked-out, Tip / End-can design, Dual Exhaust
How_To_Spot: Finish and shape of the muffler / end can
Example_Differentiates: Classic Signals & Dark have blacked-out exhaust

How_To_Spot: One continuous seat vs separate rider section
Example_Differentiates: Raider Split Seat variants; sportier trims use split seats

Possible_Values: Single-piece Seat, Split Seat, Seat with Grab Rail
How_To_Spot: Continuous vs separate raised pillion pad; grab rail type
Example_Differentiates: Distinguishes touring/comfort trims

How_To_Spot: Adjustable lever has a numbered span adjuster dial
Example_Differentiates: Premium trims add adjustable levers

How_To_Spot: Adjustable lever has a numbered span adjuster dial
Example_Differentiates: Classic Heritage (No) vs Dark/Chrome (Adjustable Levers); Meteor lever spec

How_To_Spot: Combined brake + clutch lever adjusters present
Example_Differentiates: Classic 350 2024-26 Heritage (No) vs Chrome/Emerald (Yes); Meteor Aurora/Supernova (Yes)

Possible_Values: No, Front Glove Box, External Rear Box, Under-seat Storage
How_To_Spot: Front utility box, top box mount, or boot space
Example_Differentiates: Accessory/utility trims add storage

Possible_Values: Standard, Chrome Tank, Chrome Trim / Garnish, Blacked-out
How_To_Spot: Bright chrome on tank/mudguard/exhaust vs matte blacked-out parts
Example_Differentiates: Classic Chrome vs Dark; Hunter / Bullet chrome trims

How_To_Spot: Tubular guard / crash bar around the engine and legs
Example_Differentiates: Accessory-fitted or touring trims

How_To_Spot: VIN / RC year; decides which variant generation applies
Example_Differentiates: Classic 350 generations (UCE vs J-platform)

How_To_Spot: RC / build plate month; used for mid-year refreshes
Example_Differentiates: Hunter 350 old vs new generation split within 2025
